title: The Storage Bay Where Gasoline Pools Remember

features.name: a storage structure, small and holding
features.desc-on-touch: The shed has absorbed forty-two years of vapours. You can smell decades of hands on the pump, the static between fingerprints, the slow accumulation of a task that was never completed.

features.name: a pool where gasoline remembers being touched
features.desc-on-touch: The pooled vapours rise and fall like breathing. Each surface-ripple holds the ghost-print of a different hand: callused, urgent, patient, trembling. Forty-two years of transaction, incomplete.

features.name: a stack of payment records, still pending
features.desc-on-touch: Receipts stacked in the humid air, ink fading, numbers bleeding into each other. The transaction is still waiting to be finalized. The archive received it as a .tmp file and never moved it to permanent storage.

features.name: the attendant's registry, still waiting
features.desc-on-touch: A ledger frozen mid-entry, pen still pressing. The handwriting is elegant, patient. Behind it, the wall clock holds the time when the transaction began. Neither the attendant nor you have moved since then.

inscription: the station is still waiting to complete a transaction from forty-two years ago

file.name: Pump_Receipt_Incomplete_Spectral.txt
file.note: A charge never settled, now rendered as fingerprint frequencies in the fuel vapours. The archive thinks it is memory.

reverie.title: REVERIE — petrol station at closing time
narr: She's counting the register. You stand across the
counter, watching her fingers move through bills and
coins. Neither of you speaks. The fluorescent lights
hum. Outside, the pump light is off. No more cars.
She looks up at you. Her eyes say: waiting is its own
kind of work. You nod. The day is finally complete.

exits.label: back up to the station's forecourt
toward the pit beneath the pumps where the payment still echoes
toward other stations where transactions are still incomplete
